@@ -0,0 +1,103 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: e480
+project: keyboard-navigation
+title: Cross-crate builtin-commands aggregator via linkme distributed_slice
+---
+## What
+
+Replace the hand-stitched two-crate aggregator in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs:64-71` (`default_builtin_yaml_sources` manually concatenates the commands crate and the kanban crate) with a `linkme::distributed_slice`-based registry. Each contributor crate (commands, kanban, focus, future mirdan) registers its YAML directory via a single static slice element; the aggregator iterates the slice. New contributors are wired in by adding the slice declaration in their own crate — no edits to a central aggregator.
+
+This is foundational for the `nav-jump` plan (the next task puts `nav.yaml` in `swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/`) but it stands on its own as a real architectural fix: today the aggregator at `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs` is in the wrong place — `swissarmyhammer-kanban` is a domain crate, not the right home for "compose all contributor crates' commands". After this lands, the aggregator lives where it belongs: in `swissarmyhammer-commands` (the registry crate), and every consumer (kanban-app, kanban-cli, tests) calls one function to get the full stack.
+
+### Concrete shape
+
+1. **Add `linkme` to `swissarmyhammer-commands/Cargo.toml`** as a runtime dep.
+
+2. **In `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs`**, add:
+
+   ```rust
+   /// One contributor crate's offering of builtin command YAMLs.
+   pub struct BuiltinCommandsContribution {
+       pub crate_name: &'static str,
+       /// Lower numbers load first; user overrides apply after.
+       /// Convention: 0 = generic (commands crate), 100 = subsystem
+       /// engines (focus), 200 = domain crates (kanban).
+       pub priority: u32,
+       pub sources: fn() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)>,
+   }
+
+   #[linkme::distributed_slice]
+   pub static BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS: [BuiltinCommandsContribution] = [..];
+
+   /// Flatten every contributor's YAML sources, ordered by `priority`.
+   pub fn all_builtin_yaml_sources() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)> {
+       let mut contribs: Vec<&BuiltinCommandsContribution> =
+           BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS.iter().collect();
+       contribs.sort_by_key(|c| c.priority);
+       contribs.iter().flat_map(|c| (c.sources)()).collect()
+   }
+   ```
+
+3. **In `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/lib.rs`**, register the commands crate's own contribution:
+
+   ```rust
+   #[linkme::distributed_slice(BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS)]
+   static COMMANDS_CRATE_CONTRIBUTION: BuiltinCommandsContribution =
+       BuiltinCommandsContribution {
+           crate_name: "swissarmyhammer-commands",
+           priority: 0,
+           sources: builtin_yaml_sources,
+       };
+   ```
+
+4. **In `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs`**:
+   - Keep `builtin_yaml_sources()` (the kanban-specific YAML loader).
+   - Replace the manual `default_builtin_yaml_sources()` with a thin wrapper that calls `swissarmyhammer_commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()`.
+   - Add the kanban contribution:
+
+     ```rust
+     #[linkme::distributed_slice(swissarmyhammer_commands::BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS)]
+     static KANBAN_CONTRIBUTION: BuiltinCommandsContribution =
+         BuiltinCommandsContribution {
+             crate_name: "swissarmyhammer-kanban",
+             priority: 200,
+             sources: builtin_yaml_sources,
+         };
+     ```
+   - The existing `default_commands_registry()` and `default_commands_registry_with_overrides()` continue to work — their bodies just route through `all_builtin_yaml_sources()`.
+
+5. **Verify `linkme` works through the binary entry points.** `linkme` requires the contributor crate to actually be linked into the final binary; cargo's `--gc-sections` style optimizations can drop unused statics. The standard fix is `#[used]` on the static or `linkme::distributed_slice` already handles it. Verify by:
+   - Building `kanban-app` in release mode, asserting both `swissarmyhammer-commands` and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` contributions appear in the registry.
+   - Building `kanban-cli` (which doesn't import all of kanban-app's deps) and asserting commands still load.
+   - Adding a tiny e2e test that calls `all_builtin_yaml_sources()` and asserts both `crate_name`s are present.
+
+6. **Forbid the bypass path.** Once the aggregator works, mark `swissarmyhammer-kanban::default_builtin_yaml_sources` as `#[deprecated]` or just delete it (depends on how many call sites — grep first). The single source of truth is `swissarmyhammer_commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()`.
+
+### Why this matters beyond nav-jump
+
+- The next nav-jump task adds `swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`. With this aggregator, the focus crate just adds its slice element with `priority: 100`. Without it, `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs:default_builtin_yaml_sources` has to learn about a third crate, and every future contributor crate has to land a PR against the kanban crate.
+- The aggregator currently lives in `swissarmyhammer-kanban`, which means a kanban-free consumer of `swissarmyhammer-commands` has no easy way to get the generic command stack. After this, `swissarmyhammer_commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()` is the single API regardless of which domain crates are linked.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands` exposes `BuiltinCommandsContribution` and `BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS` via `linkme::distributed_slice`.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()` returns the flattened, priority-sorted contributions and is the single API used by the kanban-app and kanban-cli.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands` registers its own contribution at priority 0.
+- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban` registers its contribution at priority 200; the manual concat in `default_builtin_yaml_sources` is removed (or it delegates).
+- [ ] `kanban-app` and `kanban-cli` both produce a registry containing both commands- and kanban-defined ids; behavior unchanged from before this refactor.
+- [ ] Adding a new contributor crate requires zero edits to existing crates — only the new crate adds its slice element.
+
+## Tests
+
+- [ ] New unit test in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs`: `all_builtin_yaml_sources_includes_self` — asserts the contribution from the commands crate itself is in the slice with `crate_name == "swissarmyhammer-commands"`.
+- [ ] New integration test in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/`: `all_builtin_sources_includes_kanban` — asserts both `swissarmyhammer-commands` and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` contributions are present, in that order, after sorting by priority.
+- [ ] Existing `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/default_commands_registry.rs` continues to pass with no changes (the registry contents are the same; only the wiring changed).
+- [ ] kanban-app smoke test: launch the registry once at app startup and assert no command id is missing relative to the prior implementation. Compare current vs. new id-set with a snapshot test.
+- [ ] Test command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — passes.
+
+## Workflow
+
+- Use `/tdd` — write the `all_builtin_yaml_sources_includes_self` and `all_builtin_sources_includes_kanban` tests first (they will fail because `BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS` doesn't exist yet); add `linkme`, the contribution struct, and the slice declarations; re-run.
\ No newline at end of file

changes.value: Cross-crate builtin-commands aggregator via linkme distributed_slice

## What

Replace the hand-stitched two-crate aggregator in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs:64-71` (`default_builtin_yaml_sources` manually concatenates the commands crate and the kanban crate) with a `linkme::distributed_slice`-based registry. Each contributor crate (commands, kanban, focus, future mirdan) registers its YAML directory via a single static slice element; the aggregator iterates the slice. New contributors are wired in by adding the slice declaration in their own crate — no edits to a central aggregator.

This is foundational for the `nav-jump` plan (the next task puts `nav.yaml` in `swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/`) but it stands on its own as a real architectural fix: today the aggregator at `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs` is in the wrong place — `swissarmyhammer-kanban` is a domain crate, not the right home for "compose all contributor crates' commands". After this lands, the aggregator lives where it belongs: in `swissarmyhammer-commands` (the registry crate), and every consumer (kanban-app, kanban-cli, tests) calls one function to get the full stack.

### Concrete shape

1. **Add `linkme` to `swissarmyhammer-commands/Cargo.toml`** as a runtime dep.

2. **In `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs`**, add:

   ```rust
   /// One contributor crate's offering of builtin command YAMLs.
   pub struct BuiltinCommandsContribution {
       pub crate_name: &'static str,
       /// Lower numbers load first; user overrides apply after.
       /// Convention: 0 = generic (commands crate), 100 = subsystem
       /// engines (focus), 200 = domain crates (kanban).
       pub priority: u32,
       pub sources: fn() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)>,
   }

   #[linkme::distributed_slice]
   pub static BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS: [BuiltinCommandsContribution] = [..];

   /// Flatten every contributor's YAML sources, ordered by `priority`.
   pub fn all_builtin_yaml_sources() -> Vec<(&'static str, &'static str)> {
       let mut contribs: Vec<&BuiltinCommandsContribution> =
           BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS.iter().collect();
       contribs.sort_by_key(|c| c.priority);
       contribs.iter().flat_map(|c| (c.sources)()).collect()
   }
   ```

3. **In `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/lib.rs`**, register the commands crate's own contribution:

   ```rust
   #[linkme::distributed_slice(BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS)]
   static COMMANDS_CRATE_CONTRIBUTION: BuiltinCommandsContribution =
       BuiltinCommandsContribution {
           crate_name: "swissarmyhammer-commands",
           priority: 0,
           sources: builtin_yaml_sources,
       };
   ```

4. **In `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs`**:
   - Keep `builtin_yaml_sources()` (the kanban-specific YAML loader).
   - Replace the manual `default_builtin_yaml_sources()` with a thin wrapper that calls `swissarmyhammer_commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()`.
   - Add the kanban contribution:

     ```rust
     #[linkme::distributed_slice(swissarmyhammer_commands::BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS)]
     static KANBAN_CONTRIBUTION: BuiltinCommandsContribution =
         BuiltinCommandsContribution {
             crate_name: "swissarmyhammer-kanban",
             priority: 200,
             sources: builtin_yaml_sources,
         };
     ```
   - The existing `default_commands_registry()` and `default_commands_registry_with_overrides()` continue to work — their bodies just route through `all_builtin_yaml_sources()`.

5. **Verify `linkme` works through the binary entry points.** `linkme` requires the contributor crate to actually be linked into the final binary; cargo's `--gc-sections` style optimizations can drop unused statics. The standard fix is `#[used]` on the static or `linkme::distributed_slice` already handles it. Verify by:
   - Building `kanban-app` in release mode, asserting both `swissarmyhammer-commands` and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` contributions appear in the registry.
   - Building `kanban-cli` (which doesn't import all of kanban-app's deps) and asserting commands still load.
   - Adding a tiny e2e test that calls `all_builtin_yaml_sources()` and asserts both `crate_name`s are present.

6. **Forbid the bypass path.** Once the aggregator works, mark `swissarmyhammer-kanban::default_builtin_yaml_sources` as `#[deprecated]` or just delete it (depends on how many call sites — grep first). The single source of truth is `swissarmyhammer_commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()`.

### Why this matters beyond nav-jump

- The next nav-jump task adds `swissarmyhammer-focus/builtin/commands/nav.yaml`. With this aggregator, the focus crate just adds its slice element with `priority: 100`. Without it, `swissarmyhammer-kanban/src/lib.rs:default_builtin_yaml_sources` has to learn about a third crate, and every future contributor crate has to land a PR against the kanban crate.
- The aggregator currently lives in `swissarmyhammer-kanban`, which means a kanban-free consumer of `swissarmyhammer-commands` has no easy way to get the generic command stack. After this, `swissarmyhammer_commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()` is the single API regardless of which domain crates are linked.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands` exposes `BuiltinCommandsContribution` and `BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS` via `linkme::distributed_slice`.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands::all_builtin_yaml_sources()` returns the flattened, priority-sorted contributions and is the single API used by the kanban-app and kanban-cli.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-commands` registers its own contribution at priority 0.
- [ ] `swissarmyhammer-kanban` registers its contribution at priority 200; the manual concat in `default_builtin_yaml_sources` is removed (or it delegates).
- [ ] `kanban-app` and `kanban-cli` both produce a registry containing both commands- and kanban-defined ids; behavior unchanged from before this refactor.
- [ ] Adding a new contributor crate requires zero edits to existing crates — only the new crate adds its slice element.

## Tests

- [ ] New unit test in `swissarmyhammer-commands/src/registry.rs`: `all_builtin_yaml_sources_includes_self` — asserts the contribution from the commands crate itself is in the slice with `crate_name == "swissarmyhammer-commands"`.
- [ ] New integration test in `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/`: `all_builtin_sources_includes_kanban` — asserts both `swissarmyhammer-commands` and `swissarmyhammer-kanban` contributions are present, in that order, after sorting by priority.
- [ ] Existing `swissarmyhammer-kanban/tests/default_commands_registry.rs` continues to pass with no changes (the registry contents are the same; only the wiring changed).
- [ ] kanban-app smoke test: launch the registry once at app startup and assert no command id is missing relative to the prior implementation. Compare current vs. new id-set with a snapshot test.
- [ ] Test command: `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-commands -p swissarmyhammer-kanban` — passes.

## Workflow

- Use `/tdd` — write the `all_builtin_yaml_sources_includes_self` and `all_builtin_sources_includes_kanban` tests first (they will fail because `BUILTIN_COMMANDS_CONTRIBUTIONS` doesn't exist yet); add `linkme`, the contribution struct, and the slice declarations; re-run.
